item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire tested as U.S. guides ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

item.title: Expert weighs in on Operation Freedom in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is holding for now despite escalated tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
item.description: Passengers aboard the ship are in good spirits but may have to quarantine for "eight weeks," a World Health Organization official told CBS News.

item.title: Consolidating your debt may not save you money this May. Here's why.
item.description: Debt consolidation sounds like a quick fix, but in this economy, it could cost you more than you bargained for.

item.title: Indiana Republicans face primary challenges after defying Trump on redistricting
item.description: Indiana Republican state senators are facing challenges during Tuesday's primary after defying President Trump's redistricting effort last year. CBS News' Fin Gomez has more.

item.title: Vance heads to Iowa for campaign event as U.S. economic concerns build
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is headed to Iowa on Tuesday for a campaign event as concerns over the U.S. economy grow amid the war with Iran. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more.

item.title: H.R. McMaster says Iranian military is in a "terrible state" as tension escalate in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump's former national security adviser and CBS News contributor, H.R. McMaster, says the Iranian military is in a "terrible state" after the standoff between the U.S. and Iran at the Strait of Hormuz escalated again on Monday.

item.title: What's next after Supreme Court's decision on redistricting
item.description: States are rethinking their congressional maps after a major Supreme Court decision on Louisiana's redistricting effort. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto explains.

item.title: Utz potato chips sold nationwide recalled over salmonella risk
item.description: Utz Quality Foods is recalling some of its Zapp's and Dirty brand potato chips because an ingredient may be contaminated by salmonella.

item.title: How anxieties in Gen Z women are being amplified and exploited
item.description: Freya India, a Substack writer who focuses on women and technology, says Gen Z women have grown up seeing themselves as "an object in a marketplace." She talks to "CBS Mornings" about how she thinks social media and tech companies have amplified the generation's anxieties and her hope for the future.

item.title: Zac Posen on how designing Kendall Jenner's Met Gala look started with his white GAP T-shirt
item.description: GAP Inc. executive vice president and creative director Zac Posen discusses designing model Kendall Jenner's dress for this year's Met Gala. He also dives into other looks that stood out at the annual event.

item.title: Best debt relief companies, plus advice borrowers need to know now
item.description: These debt relief companies could help you slash your debt, but there are some things to know before signing up.

item.title: Oprah picks "John of John" by Douglas Stuart for book club: Read an excerpt
item.description: Oprah Winfrey chose "John of John" by Douglas Stuart as her latest book club pick. Read a free excerpt here.

item.title: What would a $20,000 debt consolidation loan cost monthly at today's rates?
item.description: Before you consolidate any amount of debt, it's important to do the math and determine how much your costs will be.

item.title: Reader's guide for "John of John" by Douglas Stuart
item.description: Here's a reader's guide for "John of John" by Douglas Stuart.

item.title: UnitedHealthcare to cut prior authorization for 30% of treatments
item.description: The largest U.S. health insurer said it will eliminate approval requirements for some treatments, including select outpatient surgeries and other procedures.

item.title: Latest news as U.S. fights Iranian attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. is attempting to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz in an operation it says is separate and temporary. CBS News' Aaron Navarro, Holly Williams and Elliot Ackerman have more.

item.title: 2 hikers injured in bear attack at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: Two hikers were attacked by one or more bears, officials said, marking the first time in 2026 that a bear has injured people at Yellowstone.

item.title: Amid attacks by Trump, Justice Gorsuch says "my loyalty is to the Constitution"
item.description: President Trump has attacked Justices Neil Gorsuch and Amy Coney Barrett for voting to strike down his most sweeping tariffs.

item.title: See highlights from fashion's biggest night at Met Gala
item.description: Stars shined at fashion's biggest night of the year on Monday, the Met Gala. This year's event was co-chaired by Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman and Venus Williams. Outside the museum, protesters gathered holding letters that spelled out "tax the rich." Adriana Diaz reports.

item.title: Oprah selects "John of John" for latest book club pick
item.description: Oprah Winfrey exclusively announced on "CBS Mornings" her latest book club selection, "John of John" by Douglas Stuart, who called having his book picked by Winfrey "mind blowing."

item.title: Pennsylvania suing Character AI, claims chatbot posed as medical professional
item.description: A Character AI chatbot falsely claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist in Pennsylvania and provided an invalid license number, the state alleged.

item.title: For some older Americans, retirement today means unretiring
item.description: Nearly 20% of Americans of retirement age are employed or seeking employment. "You have to eat," said one 69-year-old of her reasons for continuing to work.

item.title: Dolly Parton shares health update after canceling Las Vegas residency
item.description: In a video posted to social media on Monday, Dolly Parton said she's been having immune and digestive issues, plus chronic kidney stones. Parton, who canceled a previously postponed Las Vegas residency, said her health is "improving every day" but she isn't performance-ready.

item.title: 2026 Tony Awards nominations are announced. See the full list.
item.description: Uzo Aduba and Darren Criss announced the 2026 Tony nominees for select categories on "CBS Mornings" on Tuesday.

item.title: Coinbase to lay off 700 workers as crypto company embraces AI
item.description: "This is a new way of working, and we need to leverage AI across every facet of our jobs," CEO Brian Armstrong said in a letter to employees.

item.title: Pentagon gives update on Iran war as Navy destroyers transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine held a presser at the Pentagon and discussed Project Freedom, a U.S. operation in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' James LaPorta and Ramy Inocencio have more.

item.title: What to know about Tuesday's primaries in Ohio and Indiana
item.description: Voters are going to the polls Tuesday in Ohio and Indiana — in the Buckeye State, they'll be casting ballots in Senate, House and governor's primary races, while Hoosiers will weigh in on House and state races.

item.title: U.K. counter-terror police investigate arson at former synagogue
item.description: Keir Starmer warned Tuesday that there would be "consequences" if Iran is proven to be behind a recent series of antisemitic attacks in the capital.

item.title: Infamous drug lord pens letters from U.S. prison, citing "cruel punishment"
item.description: Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is serving a life sentence at a maximum security facility in Colorado.
